pattern: Because every edge costs at least 10 seconds out of a small time budget, a path can only be a handful of edges long, so backtracking from node 0 while tracking which nodes have been visited (to avoid double-counting value) can afford to explore every such path.
hints: The time budget and minimum edge weight together bound how many edges a path can ever use, so a full depth-first search from node 0 is cheap enough despite the graph having up to 1000 nodes.
Track how many times the current path has visited each node so a value is only added to the running quality the first time its node is reached, and undo that bookkeeping on backtrack.
Only update the best answer when the search is standing back at node 0, since a valid path must start and end there; a path that never returns doesn't count.

edge_inputs.description: small star-shaped graph with room to visit every branch and return
single node, no edges at all
minimum time budget exactly matching one edge
a node unreachable within the graph must never contribute
a triangle whose full loop back to 0 doesn't fit the time budget
multiple routes to the same high-value node, must not double count it

stress.description: small random graphs (bounded node count and degree) with random weights and time budgets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 30))
    max_time = rng.randint(10, 100)
    values = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    degree = [0] * n
    edges = []
    edge_set = set()
    attempts = 0
    max_edges = min(2000, n * 2)
    while len(edges) < max_edges and attempts < max_edges * 10:
        attempts += 1
        u = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        v = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if u == v or degree[u] >= 4 or degree[v] >= 4:
            continue
        key = (min(u, v), max(u, v))
        if key in edge_set:
            continue
        edge_set.add(key)
        t = rng.randint(10, 100)
        edges.append([key[0], key[1], t])
        degree[u] += 1
        degree[v] += 1
    return [values, edges, max_time]
stress.note: node count kept modest (<=30) so the exhaustive DFS oracle and solution both stay fast regardless of branching
